sections.paragraph:
μέτρον, ου, τό [IE or Skt. assoc.] a measure (cp. μετρέω)

—a. w. focus on a device used to meet a standard for determination of amount or dimension Mt 7:2 and par.; Rv 21:15, 17. In imagery, of measurement for guilt Mt 23:32; of (not) doling out the spirit J 3:34; of a personal characteristic or endowment whose manner of use becomes an instrument for measuring commitment or fidelity to the will of God Ro 12:3; cp. Eph. 4:7.

—b. in transf. sense of measured extent or amount 2 Cor 10:13; Eph 4:16.
